pattern: Once every character is tagged as belonging to a word or being a separator -- with a hyphen tagged as a joiner only when both its neighbors are letters -- splitting on the separators and tallying the pieces turns every query into a single dictionary lookup.
hints: Concatenate all the chunks first; a hyphen only counts as part of a word (a joiner) when the character immediately before it and immediately after it both exist and are lowercase letters, otherwise it acts like a space and breaks the word.
Scan the concatenated string once, grouping consecutive word-characters (letters and joiner hyphens) into maximal runs -- each run is one word.
Count how many times each distinct word occurs with a hash map built once, then answer every query with a single map lookup (0 if the word never occurred).

edge_inputs.description: statement example, greeting split across chunks
statement example, joiner vs. non-joiner hyphens
statement example, boundary hyphens are never joiners
a query that never appears at all
word boundary falls exactly on a chunk boundary
only separators, no words at all

stress.description: random lowercase/space/hyphen text with random queries
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 2000))
    alphabet = 'abcde'
    chars = []
    for _ in range(n):
        r = rng.random()
        if r < 0.6:
            chars.append(rng.choice(alphabet))
        elif r < 0.8:
            chars.append(' ')
        else:
            chars.append('-')
    text = ''.join(chars)
    chunks = []
    i = 0
    while i < len(text):
        step = rng.randint(1, 5)
        chunks.append(text[i:i + step])
        i += step
    if not chunks:
        chunks = ['a']
    queries = []
    for _ in range(20):
        qlen = rng.randint(1, 4)
        q = ''.join(rng.choice(alphabet) for _ in range(qlen))
        queries.append(q)
    return [chunks, queries]

oracle_python:
import re

class Solution:
    def countWordOccurrences(self, chunks, queries):
        s = ''.join(chunks)
        marked = list(s)
        n = len(s)
        for i, c in enumerate(s):
            if c == '-':
                prev_ok = i > 0 and s[i - 1].isalpha()
                next_ok = i + 1 < n and s[i + 1].isalpha()
                if not (prev_ok and next_ok):
                    marked[i] = '#'
        s2 = ''.join(marked)
        words = re.findall(r'[a-z-]+', s2)
        from collections import Counter
        cnt = Counter(words)
        return [cnt.get(q, 0) for q in queries]

complexity.time: O(total chunk length + total query length)
